steps.title: Identify the appositive structure
steps.content: The blank contains "drummer" and "Dennis Chambers," where the name functions as an appositive renaming or clarifying the descriptor.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content:
Rule: a restrictive appositive (one that identifies which person is meant) takes no commas. "Dennis Chambers" tells us which American drummer — making it essential, not optional.

A: "American drummer, Dennis Chambers" — opening comma with no closing comma; unbalanced and incorrectly signals a nonrestrictive appositive. Incorrect.

B: "American drummer Dennis Chambers," — trailing comma only; creates an unmatched boundary that breaks the clause structure. Incorrect.

C: "American drummer Dennis Chambers" — no commas; Dennis Chambers is the essential identifier. Correct.

D: "American drummer, Dennis Chambers," — both commas signal a nonrestrictive appositive, implying the name is optional extra information. It is not optional here. Incorrect.
